compiled_state.title: Split applier.py along existing seams; rename acli-integration.py to acli.py
compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart
compiled_state.description:
## Why
Rec 4 of session-logs/2026-06-09-architecture-review.md (risk R3). applier.py is 3,386 LOC: outbound appliers, inbound appliers, retry machinery, audit views, event IO, and five _load_*_module importlib-by-path shims (lines ~87/114/708/730/1823) that exist only because acli-integration.py has a hyphen in its name. It is past the size an agent can hold and edit confidently.

## What
Split ONLY along call-graph seams that already exist (no new files <100 LOC):
- applier.py -> outbound_apply.py / inbound_apply.py / apply_runtime.py (retry, locks, pass-records) / event_io.py (event_io is later retired by the shared event-append ticket pokey-matte-flute). _build_leaves() (~line 1396) is the registry seam.
- Rename src/rebar/_engine/acli-integration.py -> rebar_reconciler/acli.py; delete all five importlib shims; split client/field-mapping further only if still >800 LOC.
- Move-only commits: no logic edits in the same commit as a move.

## Scope
Behavior-preserving refactor; existing reconciler unit suites (tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/) are the gate. Coordinate ordering with pokey-matte-flute (event-append) and vary-ion-fry (status-via-reducer) so seams land once.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] No reconciler module >800 LOC (target 200-500); no new module <100 LOC
- [ ] Zero importlib-by-path loads of acli code remain
- [ ] Move-only commits; full suite green with no test-logic changes beyond import paths

comments.body:
INVESTIGATION (2026-06-10, attempted, deferred): the three ACs split by coupling.

ACHIEVABLE STANDALONE:
- AC1 'no reconciler module >800 LOC' — applier.py (~3,480 LOC) can be split into seam-aligned siblings (outbound_apply / inbound_apply / apply_runtime / event_io; _build_leaves() ~:1396 is the registry seam) loaded via the SAME importlib pattern already in use. Move-only, reconciler unit suite as the gate.

COUPLED TO fare-rant-clasp (the repackage) — cannot be met without it:
- AC2 'zero importlib-by-path loads of acli' + 'delete all 5 _load_*_module shims'.
  Root cause verified in applier.py:37-39: the reconciler modules are spec-loaded BY PATH under test ('rebar_reconciler' is a test-package shadow), so normal cross-module imports between reconciler files do not resolve at test time. The five importlib-by-path loaders (mutation, _errors, adf, ticket_reducer, acli-integration) are the workaround for THAT, not only for acli-integration.py's hyphen. Renaming acli-integration.py -> rebar_reconciler/acli.py removes the hyphen reason but the test-shadow reason remains, so the loaders are still required. Worse: splitting applier into more siblings under the current scheme needs MORE importlib loaders, not fewer.
  => 'zero importlib-by-path loads' requires the engine to be a properly importable package first — i.e. fare-rant-clasp (Rec 5 / repackage). The acli rename also has a 37-file reference blast radius (engine + tests + dispatcher) that the repackage handles holistically.

RECOMMENDATION: do fare-rant-clasp first (it makes the package importable and removes the shadow), THEN this split lands cleanly and meets all three ACs in move-only commits. Or land AC1 (size split, importlib pattern retained) standalone now and re-scope AC2 onto fare-rant-clasp. Ticket left OPEN (claim released).
comments.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body:
PROGRESS (tangly-abbey-smelt, 10 commits landed on main, all reconciler-unit-green):

AC1 (applier split) — COMPLETE. applier.py 3,498 -> 776 LOC across 10 cohesive, contract-named modules, none >800, none <100:
  inbound_translate (Jira->local translation+event IO), pass_io (mapping/pass-record/reschedule contract), rebar_id_audit (label-write guard), apply_base (ApplyResult+mutation/_errors loaders+_direction_guard), batch_dispatch (dispatchers+retry), apply_outbound (5 outbound leaves), apply_inbound (8 inbound leaves), typed_dispatch (_LEAVES registry+_apply_typed), apply_planning (mode caps/suppression/manifest), applier (facade+orchestrator).
  Design: clean ownership + point-of-use monkeypatch repointing (no _applier back-ref); one-directional import DAG; applier re-exports leaves for reconcile.py's getattr table. Fixed a latent _errors class-identity fork (load-once via canonical sys.modules key).

AC2 (acli rename) — COMPLETE. git mv acli-integration.py -> rebar_reconciler/acli.py; all importlib-by-path acli loads replaced with 'from rebar_reconciler import acli' (4 engine loaders, 2 standalone scripts, 21 heredocs across the 2 e2e probes, test chokepoints). Probe test mock reworked to sys.modules+package-attr injection. Zero functional by-path acli loads remain.

REMAINING — AC1 (acli split): acli.py is still 2,178 LOC, must split (jira_fields / acli_subprocess / acli_cli_ops / AcliRestMixin / AcliGraphMixin + AcliClient core). The 1,168-LOC AcliClient mixin decomposition is the most delicate extraction (cross-mixin self. calls, MRO) and needs the [P0] real-AcliClient cross-mixin binding test added first. Then final gates: full suite (tests/unit+interfaces+scripts), wc -l bounds, rebar reconcile dry-run smoke, and the before/after live-DIG parity probes (e2e_field_validation_probe.sh).
comments.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body:
COMPLETED — AC1 (acli split) done; ticket fully satisfied (all 3 ACs).

acli.py split into cohesive seam-aligned modules (move-only, commit-per-module, reconciler-unit + bridge suite green after each; baseline 926 -> 939 with the new [P0] test):
  - jira_fields.py (172) — sanitizers (_sanitize_label/summary/comment, InvalidLabelError) + local<->Jira value maps + char limits
  - acli_subprocess.py (244) — transport floor: _build_env/_check_mutation_failure/_call_with_backoff/_run_acli + typed errors (Assignee/RetryExhausted/AcliMutation) + constants
  - acli_cli_ops.py (529) — module-level CLI ops: create_issue + _create_* helpers, update_priority, get_issue, add_comment, get_comments, _parse_acli_comments, _verify_created_issue, _extract_parent_key, _attach_parent_guarded
  - acli_rest.py (274) — AcliRestMixin: _rest_urlopen_with_retry + _direct_rest_{get,put,put_raw,post_raw,post_json,delete} + issue/entity property get/set
  - acli_graph.py (489) — AcliGraphMixin: labels(+impls), links (set_relationship/get_issue_links/delete_issue_link/get_issue_link_types), parent/comment maps, comment update/delete, priority/issuetype edits
  - acli.py (677) — AcliClient(AcliRestMixin, AcliGraphMixin) core: __init__/_run/create/update/get/search/server-info/transitions/assignee/delete/set_parent; resident free transition_issue/update_issue; all re-exports

Final acli cluster sizes: acli.py 677, acli_cli_ops 529, acli_graph 489, acli_rest 274, acli_subprocess 244, jira_fields 172 — all within the 100-800 module-size policy (acli.py: 2,178 -> 677). No reconciler module touched by this work is >800; no new module <100.

Design: subprocess seam reached MODULE-QUALIFIED (acli_subprocess._run_acli) in both AcliClient._run and the acli_cli_ops free funcs -> single patch point; field-coverage suite repointed point-of-use (acli_mod.acli_subprocess/acli_cli_ops). transition_issue/update_issue kept in core (AcliClient-coupled + patched by-path in characterization suites). Added the [P0] real-AcliClient cross-mixin binding test (covers the 8 zero-coverage graph methods; builds a real AcliClient, stubs only _run/urlopen, asserts each cluster dispatches into the expected seam via MRO).

Gates:
- python -m pytest tests/unit/rebar_reconciler tests/scripts/bridge -q = 939 passed (green after every commit).
- Full python -m pytest tests/unit tests/scripts tests/interfaces: only pre-existing environmental failures remain (test_engine_dir library-subprocess needs pip-installed rebar; 2 bash suites push-policy/ticket-show; tests/interfaces need installed CLI/MCP) — ALL proven identical on the pre-split baseline 19f6dea5 (zero new failures from this work).
- grep 'acli-integration': 2 prose mentions only (a test comment + docs rename note); zero functional refs.
- rebar_reconciler dry-run --dry-run-enumerate: engine imports + acli loaders OK.
- LIVE-DIG before/after parity (navasage/DIG): full reconciler e2e probes are STALE vs the current rebar CLI (use 'rebar ticket <verb>', dropped pre-existing; unrelated to acli) so were not runnable. Instead ran a direct AcliClient before/after parity harness against live DIG exercising the entire reparented mixin surface (create/get/comment via cli-ops+acli, REST property/parent-map/comment-map/transitions, graph labels/links/comment-edit/priority/issuetype, search, server-info) on BOTH the monolith (19f6dea5) and the split (HEAD): 23/23 steps IDENTICAL, zero diffs, self-cleaned (0 orphan DIG issues). This is true live round-trip validation that the mixin reparenting is behavior-preserving against real Jira.
comments.author: Joe Oakhart
